title: Trump Tries to Downplay Economic Effects of the Iran War
summary: At a White House event for Small Business Week, he described the economy as “roaring” and predicted that gas prices would go down soon.

title: Oil Prices Slide but Remain Elevated as Middle East Tensions Escalate
summary: Oil prices fell, giving back some of the gains from the previous day as attacks in the Middle East raised concerns about the war starting again.

title: As Oil Prices Stay High, China Doubles Down on Wind Power
summary: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.

title: Secret Service Shoots and Wounds Armed Man Near Washington Monument
summary: A 15-year-old boy was also hit. The motorcade of Vice President JD Vance had passed through the area shortly before the shooting.

title: Trump Administration Demands Names of 2020 Election Workers in Georgia
summary: The Justice Department again appears to be using the investigative power of the federal government to rehash debunked claims that Democrats stole the 2020 election.

title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Caribbean, Killing 2
summary: The aerial strike raised the death toll to at least 187 in the U.S. campaign against boats the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.

title: Kennedy Starts a Push to Help Americans Quit Antidepressants
summary: The health secretary has long complained that Americans overuse psychiatric medications. New policies he is introducing aim to change that.

title: Our 15 Favorite Looks at the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Regardless of the theme, these people just looked great.

title: Mingling With Jeff Bezos and Stevie Nicks at the 2026 Met Gala Cocktail Party
summary: Guests had many interpretations for the “Fashion Is Art” dress code.

title: Taiwan Could Learn From Ukraine. Informally, Connections Are Growing.
summary: The governments have no official diplomatic or military ties. But a loose network led by company executives and volunteers is bridging some of that gulf.

title: Man Accused of Starting Palisades Fire Admired Luigi Mangione, Prosecutors Say
summary: The Los Angeles suspect was lonely and angry and felt “enslaved” by rich people, prosecutors say. After being romantically spurned, they say, he went into the mountains and lit a fire.

title: New York Times Wins 3 Pulitzer Prizes
summary: The Washington Post won the award for public service, considered the most prestigious of the Pulitzers, for its coverage of the Trump administration’s overhaul of federal agencies.

title: Pulitzer Prizes: 2026 Winners List
summary: Here is the full list of winners and finalists.

title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
summary: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.

title: How a Hantavirus Infection Feels: ‘You Just Can’t Get the Air In’
summary: Doctors say that the virus, which is rare, can quickly progress from flulike symptoms to something far more serious.

title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
summary: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed two cases and are investigating four others.

title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Settle ‘It Ends With Us’ Lawsuit
summary: Ms. Lively had accused Mr. Baldoni of waging a smear campaign against her after she complained of harassment when making a film together. He had denied her accusation.

title: What a Bike Ride in Cape Town Taught Me About Apartheid
summary: A New York Times reporter joined a group of cyclists on a route meant to break down Cape Town’s lingering racial and economic barriers.

title: Elon Musk’s Lawyers Ask OpenAI’s President Why He Is Worth $30 Billion
summary: The legal team implied in a federal trial that Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president and co-founder, was driven by greed rather than building safe A.I.

title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
summary: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.

title: Supreme Court Agrees to Fast-Track Louisiana Voting Map Decision
summary: Louisiana voters who successfully challenged the state’s voting map as an illegal racial gerrymander had asked the justices to quickly return the case to the lower courts, clearing the way for a new map.

title: Streisand, LuPone, Holliday, Menzel: Broadway’s Biggest Voices
summary: Lea Michele, Christine Ebersole, Jennifer Hudson, Kristin Chenoweth, Adam Lambert and 10 others reflect on their favorite belters.

title: A Movie About Women Who Love Their Work? Groundbreaking.
summary: It turns out that some women do, in fact, dream of labor, at least the kind that comes with a sense of mastery, agency and glamour.

title: Democrats, Keir Starmer Is a Warning
summary: Keir Starmer’s collapse was written into the nature of his victory.

title: Late Night Laments Losing Spirit Airlines as a Punchline
summary: The budget carrier announced that “‘all flights have been canceled, and customer service is no longer available,’ which, coincidentally, was also Spirit’s motto,” Stephen Colbert said.

title: 1,500 Beagles From Ridglan Farms in Wisconsin Are Getting New Homes
summary: Last month, animal rights activists tried to steal some of the dogs from the Wisconsin facility, Ridglan Farms, but were thwarted by tear gas and rubber bullets.

title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
summary: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.

title: Four Epstein Victims Ask N.Y. Lawmakers to Open His Estate to Lawsuits
summary: New York State law prevents people from seeking punitive damages from the estate of someone who has died.

title: Fireworks Factory Explosion in China Kills at Least 21
summary: Dozens more were injured in the explosion, which appeared to be one of the country’s deadliest in recent years.
